title: Trim always-on runtime overhead
summary: 0.4.25 focuses on the hottest always-on playback paths by removing unconditional per-frame audio reapply work, reusing scratch lists in the main player ticks, and reducing redundant play-state polling.
reasoning: The current stability goal is smoother playback, especially when multiple instances are active. The safest first pass is to reduce repeated update costs and avoid per-frame work that is already covered by explicit mutation paths.

changes.summary: Reuses a scratch list for the standalone player runtime tick and stops reapplying audio state every frame.
changes.reasoning: These are pure maintenance reductions in the highest-frequency loop and should improve multi-instance cost without changing the user-facing playback contract.

changes.summary: Reuses a scratch list for control-surface relative binding refresh instead of allocating a new list each tick.
changes.reasoning: This removes avoidable allocation churn from another always-on maintenance loop.

changes.summary: Reuses the already-read play state inside the main player tick instead of re-querying `VideoPlayer.isPlaying` multiple times in the same pass.
changes.reasoning: This keeps the hot loop a little leaner and avoids duplicate runtime checks.

verification: Stamp version 0.4.25 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs, then build the player release in package-only mode.
Verify the packaged artifact is named FrameAngel.Player.0.4.25.var and contains the 0.4.25 asset, plugin, preset, and demo scene.
Verify the player plugin compiles after the tick-path maintenance changes.
knownIssues: This slice is intentionally bounded to the existing hot loops and does not yet change larger media-transition behavior or input-path design.
Live VaM proof is still needed to judge how much the maintenance pass improves multi-instance playback.
